description: Global stopwords — known placeholder/test values. Case-insensitive. Must be specific enough to avoid suppressing real data. Single common words (test, example) are too broad for global — use per-pattern stopwords instead. Entries may use the `xor:<base64>` or `b64:<base64>` prefix to obfuscate credential-shaped strings that GitHub push protection would otherwise reject — these are decoded at load time by data_classifier.patterns._decoder. The encoded entries below cover, in order: two Stripe docs test keys (sk_test / pk_test, 32 chars each), GitLab PAT, Anthropic API key (sk-ant-api03), OpenAI project key (sk-proj), Hugging Face token (hf_), and npm publish token (npm_). Encoding is defensive — all-zero bodies also pass push protection today, but encoding everything exercises the decode path and keeps the file stable if scanner rules tighten.
